item_spec.concrete_change:
Surface the resolved audit scope at first contact and have the loader confirm it before the run commits, so a mis-resolved repo_root is caught early (meta-audit-log O3: a cwd accident silently scoped the audit to 1 of 3 workspaces and turned off git signals, with nothing forcing confirmation before .audit-artifacts was written). Changes (DISCOVERY TARGETS — locate the repo_root/scope resolution in the audit-code wrapper or intake layer, and the /audit-code loader source template, during the implement phase):

(1) Backend (wrapper/intake scope resolution): when scope is resolved for a run, produce a concise scope summary — resolved repo_root (absolute), auditable file count, git availability (yes/no), and, for monorepos, whether the resolved root looks like a sub-package. Emit it in the first step's output (and/or a small scope-summary artifact) BEFORE the run fans out into heavy artifact generation. Add MIS-SCOPE SMELL detection that flags the exact O3 failure: (a) the resolved root has no .git but an ANCESTOR directory does (git_unavailable_but_ancestor_is_repo) — the strongest signal of accidental sub-directory scoping; (b) the resolved root is a workspace member of a parent monorepo (parent package.json declares workspaces that include it). Carry these flags in the scope summary.

(2) /audit-code loader template: instruct the host to echo the resolved scope to the user as 'Auditing <repo_root>, <N> files, git: yes/no — proceed?' and to REQUIRE confirmation before continuing when any mis-scope smell is set (git unavailable while an ancestor is a repo, or the root is a monorepo sub-package). On normal, unambiguous scope the echo can be informational rather than blocking.

Keep the resolution behavior itself unchanged — this adds visibility + a gate, not a re-scoping heuristic. Edit the loader SOURCE template (rendered by `ensure`), not generated output. CROSS-REFERENCE: this pairs with FINDING-011 (the cwd footgun that CAUSED the mis-scope) — both reduce the chance of an accidental sub-directory audit.

tests_to_write.name: scope resolution emits a summary and detects mis-scope smells
assertions: Given a directory that is itself a git repo root with auditable files, the scope summary reports the resolved repo_root, a correct auditable file count, and git: yes, with no mis-scope smell.
Given a sub-directory whose .git lives at an ANCESTOR, the summary reports git: no and sets the git_unavailable_but_ancestor_is_repo flag.
Given a workspace member of a monorepo (parent package.json with workspaces), the summary sets the sub-package flag.

tests_to_write.name: scope summary is produced before heavy artifact generation
assertions: The scope summary is available from the first step output (or scope-summary artifact) prior to the bulk of .audit-artifacts being written, so a host can confirm before committing.
When no smell is detected the summary is informational; when a smell is set it is marked as requiring confirmation.
